title: Custom Domain Onboarding — automate Cloudflare Pages domain attach + SSL polling
problem: Each buyer wants their app at their own domain (e.g. roger-voice-truck.com). Cloudflare Pages supports unlimited custom domains per project via the HTTP API. To keep onboarding under 5 minutes of operator time, this feature wraps the CF API in: (a) a one-call domain-attach when a tenant is provisioned, (b) generated CNAME instructions emailed to the buyer, (c) a polling job that watches SSL provisioning state and updates tenants.status from 'pending' to 'active', (d) operator-console signals for the failure modes (domain conflict, DNS misconfig, SSL stuck). Without this automation, every onboarding requires Marc to log into the CF dashboard and click through the domain-add UI — which negates the 5-minute provisioning promise.
analysis: Wraps the Cloudflare Pages API in a thin helper used by the operator's fleet wizard. Today /admin/fleet/new inserts a tenants row and a primary tenant_domains row (status=pending), then displays manual instructions: operator goes to the CF dashboard, adds the custom domain there, and asks the buyer to update DNS. This feature collapses the dashboard step into an API call made automatically when the wizard succeeds. CF Pages exposes POST /accounts/{id}/pages/projects/{p}/domains to attach a custom domain to a project; CF then handles SSL provisioning automatically once DNS resolves to the project's CNAME target. Errors from the API are surfaced to the operator inline; the tenant row still commits regardless (re-attach is idempotent later).

decisions.decision: CF API call is best-effort, not transactional with the tenant insert
decisions.rationale: If the API call fails (rate limit, transient network, expired token) we don't want to roll back the tenant — the buyer's data + theme are independent of the domain registration with CF. A failed API call leaves a 'pending' tenant_domains row that the operator can retry from the fleet view. Coupling them would force the operator to redo more work on transient failures.
alternativesConsidered: Two-phase commit with rollback on API failure (more complex, no real safety win)
Synchronous-only: refuse to commit the tenant if CF API fails (worse UX for transient errors)

decisions.decision: API token stored as a Pages Functions env var, not in D1
decisions.rationale: Tokens are credentials. Cloudflare's encrypted env vars at the Pages level are the right home; D1 is for app data. No code path reads the token outside the cloudflare.ts helper, and it's never serialized to a response.
alternativesConsidered: Per-tenant tokens in tenants.flags (lets buyers BYO their own CF account, but inverts the operator-managed model)

decisions.decision: SSL status starts as 'pending'; operator-manual refresh later (no cron yet)
decisions.rationale: CF takes 5–10 min to provision SSL after DNS resolves — the operator's natural cadence covers this without an automated poller. A retry button in the fleet view would give a manual refresh; a cron'd poll is a Phase 4 ergonomic upgrade that doesn't gate the first-buyer ship.
alternativesConsidered: Workers cron trigger that polls every 5 min (premature)
Webhook from CF (not offered for this event)

implementation: functions/_lib/cloudflare.ts wraps the CF API: addCustomDomain(env, domain) and listCustomDomains(env), both authenticated via CF_API_TOKEN against /accounts/{CF_ACCOUNT_ID}/pages/projects/{CF_PAGES_PROJECT_NAME}/domains. functions/api/admin/fleet.ts POST handler is updated to call addCustomDomain *after* the tenant + tenant_domains rows commit; failure logs and reports a 'partial-success' field in the response payload, leaving the rows in place. wrangler.toml gains comments documenting the three required env vars (CF_API_TOKEN, CF_ACCOUNT_ID, CF_PAGES_PROJECT_NAME). When any of these is missing (dev environment, unconfigured prod), the helper returns { skipped: true } and the wizard proceeds with the existing manual-fallback messaging.
successCriteria: Provisioning a buyer in /admin/fleet/new with all three CF env vars set causes the buyer's domain to appear in the CF Pages dashboard's Custom Domains list within seconds, without any dashboard interaction by the operator. Failures (invalid domain, conflict, missing token) surface clearly in the wizard's success screen, leaving the tenant + tenant_domains rows intact for retry.
userGuide: When you click Provision in the new-buyer wizard, the buyer's domain is registered with Cloudflare automatically. You'll see the result on the success screen — either 'Domain registered, SSL provisioning' or a clear error you can retry from the fleet list. You no longer need to add the domain manually in the Cloudflare dashboard for the typical happy path.
knownLimitations: No SSL-status polling — ssl_status stays 'pending' until manually refreshed (or the future cron poller is built in Phase 4).
No 'remove domain from CF' flow when freezing a tenant — the row is just marked frozen; CF still has the domain registered. Cleanup is a manual operator step.
API call failures don't auto-retry — operator clicks Retry on the fleet row to retry.

revisions.author: marc+claude
revisions.reason: Initial fill ahead of build — captures the best-effort vs transactional decision and the SSL-polling deferral.
